# small-language-models

Small language models derive personality and capability from constraints rather than scale; 2-bit quantization and specialized architectures force intentional design choices. Recent Qwen 3.5 results demonstrate domain-specific fine-tuning dramatically outperforms large generalist models (93.5% vs 80.5%), validating a mushi v2 direction combining small models with confidence-aware mechanisms and structured memory.
